  "findings": [
    "Roofing nails used in joist hangers instead of manufacturer-specified structural connectors",
    "Incorrect fastener type identified by large head diameter and smooth shank pattern",
    "Fasteners lack required shear capacity for joist hanger application"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove all roofing nails from joist hangers",
    "Replace with Simpson-approved structural connectors (e.g., 10d common nails or SD screws per catalog)",
    "Inspect all hanger connections for proper fastener quantity and pattern before closing out"
  ],
  "rationale": "Joist hangers require specific fasteners per manufacturer instructions (Simpson catalogs) and IRC R502.6. Roofing nails have insufficient shear strength and are not approved for structural connections, creating a high risk of hanger failure under load.",

    "IRC R502.6",
    "Simpson Strong-Tie Fastening Systems catalog",
    "Manufacturer installation instructions for joist hangers"
  ]
